incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong category on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Count the symbols in each column and look for the one with fewer than 4.

incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong category on second attempt

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Fewer than 4 means 3, 2, 1, or 0. Count each column's symbols. Which one has 3?

incorrects.description: Student clicked a wrong category three or more times — system models the answer

params.description: System counts symbols in each column, highlighting Elephants with 3 symbols

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Fewer than 4 means less than 4. Lions has 5 — that's more. Monkeys has 7 — more. Elephants has 3 — that's fewer than 4. Penguins has 6 — more. The answer is Elephants.
